epigraph.text: For every complex problem, there is a solution that is simple, neat, and wrong.

follyOfTheDay.headline: BANK ROBBER ATTEMPTS AUTO-MONEY-LAUNDERING, RETURNS WITH RECEIPT
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A 29-year-old Nashville resident has provided law enforcement with an instructive case study in the intersection of criminal ambition and spectacular failure. After withdrawing $8,000 from First National Bank—an act which, it bears noting, requires no subterfuge whatsoever—the subject then returned to the same institution two hours later to deposit $4,000 into his personal account.

When questioned, the perpetrator explained that he believed this transaction constituted legitimate 'money laundering,' apparently having confused the phrase with a straightforward mathematical operation. Bank records confirm that the defendant helpfully provided his identification, allowed security footage to document his activities, and established a clear paper trail of both the theft and the subsequent deposit.

Federal prosecutors have expressed gratitude for the defendant's unwitting cooperation with their investigation.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: A man achieves the remarkable feat of incriminating himself more thoroughly than any investigator could manage, then appears baffled when this proves inadvisable.
